differential_diagnoses: PASH Syndrome (Pyoderma gangrenosum, Acne, and Suppurative Hidradenitis)
Systemic Inflammatory Response Syndrome (SIRS) secondary to underlying occult inflammatory process
Infectious cellulitis/necrotizing soft tissue infection (ruled out by lack of culture growth and antibiotic response)
next_steps: Punch biopsy of the leading edge of an ulcer for histopathology (to distinguish neutrophilic dermatosis from infection and vasculitis)
Cultures of skin biopsy specimen (aerobic, anaerobic, fungal, and mycobacterial) to definitively rule out occult infection
Colonoscopy to evaluate for underlying inflammatory bowel disease
Initiation of systemic high-dose glucocorticoid therapy (e.g., prednisone or methylprednisolone) pending results of infection screens
Wound care consultation for non-adherent dressings and management of pathergy-prone wounds
Follow-up transthoracic echocardiography to monitor the resolution of right-sided heart dilation and cardiac strain
